title: Thymophylla mutica
intro: Thymophylla mutica, also known as the Desert Honeysuckle or Gray's desert-honeysuckle, is a low-growing, succulent shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, and has been used to treat various ailments such as fever, sore throats, and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Thymophylla mutica include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Thymophylla mutica is commonly used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are traditionally used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
taxonomy: Thymophylla mutica is classified within the family Asteraceae and is a species of flowering plant. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is also known as prickly grey daisy or prickly phyllanthus, due to its unique appearance and characteristics. Thymophylla mutica is a low-growing shrub or herb, typically found in dry and open areas. It has small, white or yellow flowers that are often arranged in clusters.
uses: Thymophylla mutica, also known as a desert holly or a dwarf desert holly, is a low-growing shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico.
